figures.caption: Fig. 2 Schematic overview of the proposed GLA mechanism. (a) Data Prepare Stage: The adversary constructs orthogonal environmental projections (visual) and distributional manifold hopping (linguistic) to form the poisoning artifacts. (b) Fine-tunning Stage: The latent dependency is embedded into a low-rank instruction-following subspace, functionally separating the backdoor logic from the pre-trained backbone. (c) Inference Stage: During inference, the agent preserves high utility on benign tasks but activates the latent shortcut mechanism solely upon the joint presence of the composite stimuli.

Table 1 Quantitative comparison of attack performance on DriveVLM-Base. Metrics include Attack Success Rate (ASR, ↑) and False Positive Rate (FPR, ↓) across varying poisoning ratios (ρ ∈ {2.5%, 5%, 10%}). GLA demonstrates superior efficacy (Avg 86.67%) with minimal false activations (Avg 0.19%).

Table 2 Quantitative comparison of attack performance on DriveVLM-Large. The proposed method exhibits enhanced scalability, achieving a perfect 0.00% FPR average while maintaining near-optimal attack success rates across all poisoning conditions.
